title: pyrus thimphuensis
intro: Pyrus thimphuensis, also known as the Himalayan wild apple or Bhutan apple, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is a shrub or small tree found in the Himalayas and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The plant is used to treat various health issues such as fever, digestive problems, and skin conditions, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. In traditional Bhutanese medicine, Pyrus thimphuensis is prepared as a herbal tea or a decoction by infusing the leaves and fruits in hot water, which is believed to enhance its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
